figures_detected.description: Timing chain mating marks diagram showing silver and bronze mating marks on camshaft sprockets, with link counts: 5 links, 7 links, 11 links between marks
Camshaft alignment showing knock pins on both camshafts aligned as required for installation
Tensioner assembly components: tensioner body, piston, ratchet, spring, plunger
Hand assembling tensioner by rotating and pushing ratchet up the spiral with piston and ratchet labelled
Installed tensioner on timing chain assembly showing tensioner position on engine

notes: Set timing chain by aligning mating marks with those of camshaft sprockets.
Make sure sprocket's mating marks face engine front.
Make sure camshafts are aligned as shown in figure.

steps.text: Assemble the tensioner by rotating and pushing the ratchet up the spiral until it stops. The tensioner is now pre-set. Chain lash will automatically set the tensioner when the engine is started. Install the tensioner.

content_blocks.text:
CAUTION:
• Check no problems occur when engine is rotated.
• Make sure that No. 1 piston is set at T.D.C. on its compression stroke.
